title: dryopteris subcrenulata
intro: Dryopteris subcrenulata, also known as the curled strap fern or subcrenulate wood fern, is a type of fern native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Dryopteris subcrenulata can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
